rulings.Case Title: RAY BRONNER VS. RICHARD S KAIHARA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 2, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFF RAY BRONNER Motion For Judgment. Denied. Defendants Jeff Lance and Hayman Brothers, Inc. filed a verified amended answer on April 16, 2012. While defendants' answers are conclusory, plaintiff's remedy is to pursue discovery to determine the viability of the defenses. Given the strong policy of deciding cases on the merits, it would be improper to enter defaults against the defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. PAUL JUN KIM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 2, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORP VS. JAMES P QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulatuion Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 2, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Enforce Stipulatuion Injunction. Granted. Plaintiffs have shown that Defendants willfully violated the Stipulated Injunction entered on October 11, 2011. Defendants are ordered to immediately comply with all terms of the Stipulated Injunction with documentary proof of compliance provided to Plaintiffs. Defendants are also encouraged to continue their efforts to sell the property. Plaintiffs are entitled to payment of $11,600 for their attorney's fees which must be paid within 60 days or through an escrow for the sale of the building, whichever occurs earlier. Defendants are further ordered to pay Plaintiffs $100,000 in civil penalties for violating the Stipulated Injunction. However, the $100,000 is stayed for 60 days pending defendants' successful efforts to sell the building and their documented compliance with all the terms of the Stipulated Injunction. If the property has been sold or is in escrow with all conditions waived and/or defendants are in full compliance with the Stipulated Injunction withinn 60 days, the civil penalties will be permanently stayed. If the parties disagree whether defendants are in full compliance with the Stipulated Injunction, the court, upon ex parte application after the parties have met and conferred, will appoint a referee to decide that issue in the first instance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Melvin Moran Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cgc-11-516344 With Case Cgc-10-501463.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 2, 2012, Line 8. Defendant Melvin Moran Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) CGC-11-516344 With Case CGC-10-501463. Granted. Judicial economy, convenience of witnesses and avoidance of possibly inconsistent rulings warrant that these two very similar lawsuits be consolidated for expert discovery and trial purposes. The trial date of June 11 in case 501463 is vacated. Trial for both cases is now August 27, 2012 at 9:30am in department 206. Percipient discovery deadlines in case 501463 are not changed by this order. Expert discovery deadlines in case 501463 now track the new trial date. The lead case is 501463 and all further filings will be in that case only. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Alfonso And Martha Picazo'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Subpoena For Tax Records Or In The Alternative To Modify The Subpoena And Issued A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 2, 2012, Line 10, Defendants Alfonso And Martha Picazo's Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Subpoena For Tax Records Or In The Alternative To Modify The Subpoena And Issued A Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED without prejudice to defendants' right to re-notice the motion. Defendants served the moving papers 12 court-days before the hearing. Code of Civil Procedure Section 1005(b) requires service of the moving papers at least 16 court-days prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BUNZL DISTRIBUTION USA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Protective Order; Request
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 02, 2012, Line 31. DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S Notice Of Motion And motion For Protective Order. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro as follows: Plaintiff's Motion is to Compel is Granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's motion for protective order is denied in part and granted in part. Defendant is to provide a privilege log which includes all documents that are included in the file at the FTB's settlement bureau. Defendant is also to amend answers where the deliberative process privilege is claimed and provide documents which deal with the cost-benefit analysis made by the FTB regarding Bunzl's tax protest. FTB's request for a protective order is granted. Bunzl's requests shall be limited to that information which is relevant to the FTB's compliance with Revenue and Taxation Code section 19442 only. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVGENI LITVAK et al VS. YURI GALASHOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 2, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT YURI GALASHOV's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses To Impose Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiffs Zinoviy Litvak, Raisa Galanter, and cross-defendant Svetlana Samarina all have failed to respond to the discovery propounded by Cross-Complainant Yuri Galashov. All objections have therefore been waived. Within 15 days of this order Plaintiffs' Zinoviy LItvak, Raisa Galanter, and cross-defendant Svetlana Samarina shall all respond without objection to Yuri Galeshov's From Interrogatories - General, Set One. They shall also all respond to and produce documents in response to Defendant and Cross-Complainant Yuri Galashov's Request for Production of Documents, Set One. The court awards sanctions in the amount of $412.50 against Zinoviy Litvak, $412.50 against Raisa Galanter, and $412.50 against Svetlana Samarina. All sanctions shall be paid to Defendant and Cross-Complainant Yuri Galashov within ten days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
